segments.p2-9-g314: "И" is the conjunction 'and' linking to what came before; "все время" is an adverbial phrase 'all the time/throughout'; "потом" means 'afterward' or 'then' and places the action in time; "она" is the subject 'she'; "слушала" is past feminine of слушать ('listened/was listening to'); "меня" is the accusative 'me' (object). The whole clause states that she kept listening to me during that whole subsequent period.
segments.p2-9-g315: "раскрыв" is a perfective adverbial participle (деепричастие) from раскрыть: 'having opened' or idiomatically 'with (her) mouth open'; it modifies the main verb and shows concurrent action; "рот" is the direct object 'mouth' in the accusative, so the phrase describes her posture (mouth open) while listening.
segments.p2-9-g316: "открыв" is another adverbial participle (from открыть) 'having opened' and governs "глаза" (accusative plural 'eyes'), describing that she opened her eyes; "и" links this participle to the next participle; "дрожа" is the present adverbial participle of дрожать 'trembling' (imperfective) indicating simultaneous ongoing trembling; "от" is the preposition 'from/because of' requiring the genitive; "ужасного" is genitive singular of ужасный ('terrible') modifying "страха" (genitive 'of fear'), so together 'trembling from terrible fear.'
segments.p2-9-g317: "Цинизм" is a nominative noun 'Cynicism,' presented alone for emphasis (a headline-like topical word), introducing the theme that follows.
segments.p2-9-g318: The repeated "цинизм" is the grammatical subject ('the cynicism'), "моих" is the genitive plural of 'my' modifying "слов" (genitive plural of 'word/utterance') — together 'the cynicism of my words'; "придавил" is past masculine singular of придавить ('pressed down'/'crushed'), agreeing with the masculine subject, and "ее" is the accusative 'her' (object). The verb is used figuratively: the speaker's cynical tone overwhelmed or crushed her emotionally.
segments.p2-9-g276: Да here is an emphatic affirmative particle / interjection placed before the verb to confirm or stress what follows (roughly 'yes' or 'indeed'). It does not change form for person or tense and simply intensifies the speaker's admission.
segments.p2-9-g277: Смеялся is the past tense, masculine singular form of смеяться (imperfective in form but used as a simple past admission). It describes the speaker's action 'laughed' (in this exclamation the imperfective past simply states that he laughed); word order is subject (implied) + predicate with the explicit particle Да before it.
segments.p2-9-g278: This clause foregrounds the accusative pronoun Меня for emphasis: Меня оскорбили = 'I was insulted' (a verbal action affecting the speaker). Перед тем functions as 'before that' / 'previously' (перед + demonstrative тем) and modifies the timing; за обедом is an adverbial specifying time ('during/at dinner'). Вот те is a pointing phrase 'those very (ones),' identifying the agents who did the insulting.
segments.p2-9-g279: Которые introduces a relative clause modifying те ('those who...'). Тогда gives the temporal setting 'then,' and передо мной ('in front of me; in my presence') uses the preposition перед (here with the colloquial contracted form передо) + instrumental мной to indicate that the arrival happened before/near the speaker. Приехали is past plural 'they arrived/came.'
segments.p2-9-g280: Я приехал к вам is a straightforward past tense clause 'I came to you' (приехал = arrived/came, к + dative marks the direction 'to you'). С тем here begins a purposive construction 'with the (intention)...' and sets up the following infinitive clause (чтоб ...).
segments.p2-9-g281: Чтоб introduces a purpose clause meaning 'in order to.' Исколотить is a perfective verb meaning to beat/maul severely here (a violent, completed action intended). Одного из них is the direct object phrase 'one of them' (из + plural pronoun = 'one of').
segments.p2-9-g282: Офицера is the accusative singular of офицер ('an officer') and specifies the intended target of the planned assault; as an animate masculine noun the form совпадает with the genitive shape but functions here as the direct object.
segments.p2-9-g283: Но не удалось is an impersonal past construction meaning 'but it didn't work out' or 'I couldn't (do it).' Не negates удалось (удаться = to succeed/manage), so the speaker reports failure of the plan.
segments.p2-9-g284: Не застал is a short past statement 'I didn't catch/find (him)' — here застал (застать) means 'to find someone (at a place)' or 'to catch up with.' The object is omitted but understood (the officer).
segments.p2-9-g285: Надо же было ... выместить is an impersonal past necessity: надо было = 'it was necessary' or 'I had to.' Же is an emphatic particle strengthening the assertion. Обиду на ком-нибудь выместить uses обиду ('a hurt/resentment') with на + кого-нибудь ('on someone') and the infinitive выместить 'to vent (it) on' — meaning he had to take revenge on some other person.
segments.p2-9-g286: Свое взять is an idiomatic infinitive phrase 'to take one's own / to get one's own back' (свое = 'one's own [revenge/dues]', взять = 'take'). It states the speaker's aim to recover or avenge himself.
segments.p2-9-g287: Ты подвернулась is past tense, feminine singular of the reflexive verb подвернуться 'to present oneself / to come up (by chance)'; here it means 'you happened to be there / you turned up' (the addressee is the incidental target). The personal pronoun ты marks the direct address.
segments.p2-9-g288: Я над тобой и вылил зло и насмеялся combines actions in the past: я (I) + над тобой (над + instrumental тобой = 'over/at you' indicating the target) + вылил зло ('poured out malice' — metaphorically vented bad feelings) + и насмеялся ('and laughed at you' — насмеяться is a perfective verb meaning to mock or ridicule). The sequence states completed actions directed at the addressee.
segments.p2-9-g289: Меня унизили is a passive-styled construction with the accusative object Меня placed first for emphasis: 'They humiliated me' (унизили = past plural of унизить, 'to humiliate').
segments.p2-9-g290: Так и я хотел унизить links the previous clause to the speaker's reaction: так = 'so/thus,' и adds emphasis, я хотел унизить = 'I wanted to humiliate (in turn)' — хотел is past of хотеть (to want) followed by the infinitive унизить.
segments.p2-9-g291: Меня в тряпку растерли is an idiomatic, figurative past construction: меня (accusative) + в тряпку (literally 'into a rag') + растерли (past plural of растереть, 'rub/beat/crush thoroughly') — together 'they ground me down/beat me to a pulp' (metaphorical image).
segments.p2-9-g292: Так и я власть захотел показать repeats the pattern 'so I too...' (так и), with я захотел показать ('I wanted to show/demonstrate') where власть ('power/authority') is the object: 'so I wanted to show power' — an intention to assert authority in response.
segments.p2-9-g333: Мораль is a bare noun functioning as the (implicit) direct object of the infinitive читать — a clipped, brusque way of naming the thing to be read: “the moral”/“a moral (lesson).” Word order (noun before the particles and verb) gives it a pointed, quoted tone.
segments.p2-9-g334: что ли is the conversational particle что + enclitic ли that softens or turns the phrase into a rhetorical question: “…or what?/…is that it?” It does not introduce a subordinate clause here but merely qualifies the preceding noun-phrase as a suggestion or sneer.
segments.p2-9-g335: читать is the infinitive “to read,” used here as the main verbal nucleus of the short question (infinitive question with particle что ли). The question is formed by intonation and the particle rather than by a finite verb form.
segments.p2-9-g336: Власти is the noun власть in the genitive (or part of an elided list) used with the impersonal надо/надо было construction: with надо it appears in the genitive to name what is needed (‘(of) power’). The repeated single word, followed by a comma, is emphatic.
segments.p2-9-g337: власти мне надо было тогда is the full impersonal past construction: мне (dative) + надо было (impersonal past of надо) = “I needed”; власти is the thing needed in the genitive. Тогда places the need in the past context (“then”).
segments.p2-9-g338: игры было надо repeats the same impersonal pattern with игры in the genitive: ‘(a) game/play was needed’ — here meaning he wanted some sort of game or play (a manipulative or amusing episode). Было makes the construction past/remote.
segments.p2-9-g339: слез твоих надо было добиться continues the pattern: слез твоих (genitive plural with possessive) is the object he needed, надо было добиться = ‘it was necessary to obtain/win’ (добиться + genitive). The verb добиться is perfective and names the required action (‘to succeed in getting’).
segments.p2-9-g340: унижения appears as a noun in the same elliptic list of things he wanted; here it stands as a nominal object (‘humiliation’), parallel to the previous genitives.
segments.p2-9-g341: истерики твоей - вот чего надо мне было тогда! is an emphatic summation. истерики твоей (genitive with possessive) = ‘your hysterics’; вот чего is a demonstrative+interrogative-like phrase meaning ‘that’s what…’, and надо мне было тогда repeats the impersonal ‘I needed (that) then.’ The dash heightens the exclamatory force.
segments.p2-9-g342: Я ведь и сам тогда не вынес is built around the past verb вынес (вынести) meaning ‘to endure/stand.’ The structure я … не вынес is simple past negation ‘I couldn’t stand it’; ведь and сам add emphasis (‘after all’ / ‘even I myself’), and тогда situates it in that moment.
segments.p2-9-g343: потому что я дрянь is the causal clause introduced by потому что ‘because’: я дрянь = ‘I’m scum/filth’ (self‑insult). The clause explains the preceding inability to endure.
segments.p2-9-g344: перепугался и черт знает для чего дал тебе сдуру адрес is a sequence of past actions and an idiom. Перепугался = ‘got frightened/panicked’; и links to the next clause; черт знает для чего = idiomatic ‘the devil (or God) knows for what reason’ (i.e. ‘who knows why’); дал тебе (прош. вр. дать) = ‘gave you’ with тебе dative; сдуру is an adverb ‘foolishly/in a panic’; адрес is the direct object ‘(my) address.’
segments.p2-9-g345: Так я потом is a linking phrase: так ‘so/thus’ frames the following action as a consequence, я = ‘I’, потом = ‘later’ — which together set the temporal/consequential frame for what follows.
segments.p2-9-g346: еще домой не дойдя is a perfective gerund (деепричастие) construction: не дойдя (‘not having reached/arrived’) with домой (‘home, as a direction’) and ещё (‘yet/still’) gives the sense ‘before I’d even got home’ — a temporal clause describing when the main action happened.
segments.p2-9-g347: уж тебя ругал на чем свет стоит за этот адрес is the main clause with an idiom. уж is an intensifier (‘already/indeed’), тебя = direct object ‘you,’ ругал = past of ругать ‘scold, tear into.’ На чем свет стоит is a fixed idiom meaning ‘with all possible force’ / ‘to no end’ (literally ‘on whatever the world stands’). За этот адрес = ‘about/for that address’ identifies the cause of the scolding.
segments.p2-9-g321: One-word exclamation using the infinitive спасать. Russian often uses the infinitive as an urgent cry or command here (functioning like an imperative). The form is non‑finite (infinitive) rather than a conjugated verb.
segments.p2-9-g322: продолжал is past imperfective, masculine singular, narrative past: “I continued / I went on.” The subject я follows the verb here (common in spoken/narrative style) and the comma marks the reporting clause for direct speech.
segments.p2-9-g323: A string of adverbial participles and phrases: вскочив is a perfective adverbial participle (деепричастие) indicating a single completed action prior to or at the start of the main action (“having jumped up” / “springing up”); бегая is an imperfective adverbial participle showing an ongoing simultaneous action (“running/about”); со стула = “from/off the chair” (со is the variant of с used before certain consonant clusters); перед ней взад и вперед по комнате = spatial phrase “in front of her, back and forth across the room.”
segments.p2-9-g324: От чего спасать is a rhetorical exclamation built from the preposition от + interrogative чьего/чего in the genitive and the infinitive спасать as the main predicate; literally “from what (to) save” = “save me from what? / what is there to save me from?” The infinitive again functions as an exclamatory main verb.
segments.p2-9-g325: Да here is an emphatic/colloquial particle (roughly “why/well/indeed” in this context) preceding the subject; я is the pronoun subject “I.” The comma shows a short pause before the following parenthetical.
segments.p2-9-g326: может is a modal particle (from мочь) used colloquially to mean “maybe / perhaps / might” and softens the claim that follows.
segments.p2-9-g327: Elliptical predicative clause: сам is an emphatic particle meaning “even / myself,” placed before the object for emphasis; тебя is the direct object “you”; хуже is the comparative adjective “worse,” functioning as the predicative. The copula is omitted (common in Russian), so the phrase reads “I may even be worse than you.”
segments.p2-9-g328: A colloquial rhetorical question: Что functions as an exclamatory particle (= “why / what / why on earth” here); ты — subject “you”; мне — indirect object “to me” (here: throw at me); тогда же is “right then / at that very moment” (же adds emphasis); не + кинула is negated past feminine perfective “didn’t throw” (implying she was the one who could have thrown it); в рожу is crude colloquial “in the face” (рожа is pejorative for face).
segments.p2-9-g329: когда introduces the subordinate clause “when …”; я is the subject; тебе is the indirect object “to you”; рацеи-то here is the direct object with the emphatic particle -то attached (the lexical item рацеи is presented in the source data as the object being read — the -то emphasizes ‘that very thing’); читал is past imperfective “I was reading / I read,” and the colon that follows introduces quoted speech.
segments.p2-9-g330: Beginning of the quoted question: А is the coordinating particle/conjunction “and / but,” here starting the quotation; ты is the vocative/subject “you.” This opens the reported question.
segments.p2-9-g331: мол is a quotative particle meaning roughly “they say / as if / so they put it,” signaling that the speaker is quoting or paraphrasing what others said (often with ironic distance).
segments.p2-9-g332: сам is an emphatic particle here (“yourself / yourself even”), placed before the question word; зачем is the interrogative adverb “why / what for”; к нам is the prepositional phrase “to us / to our place” (к + dative); зашел is past tense perfective masculine “you came in / dropped by.” Together they form the quoted direct question “Why did you yourself come to us?”
segments.p2-9-g348: Я is the subject pronoun 'I'. Уж is an emphatic particle meaning 'already' (often after the subject). Ненавидел is the past tense, masculine singular form of ненавидеть ('to hate'), so the clause is a straightforward past simple SVO construction; тебя is the accusative object 'you'.
segments.p2-9-g349: Потому что is the causal conjunction 'because'. The second clause repeats the subject я for emphasis; тебе is the dative 'to you' (лгать takes a dative to indicate the person lied to). Тогда is the time adverb 'then', and лгал is the masculine past-tense form 'lied'.
segments.p2-9-g350: Потому что 'because' introduces the explanation. Я is repeated for emphasis. Только 'only/merely' limits what follows. На словах is a fixed phrase 'in words/only verbally' (на + plural noun), and поиграть is a prefixed infinitive 'to play for a while' (here part of an elliptical construction: the verb 'want' is implied rather than stated).
segments.p2-9-g351: В голове is literal 'in (the) head' but idiomatically 'in my head/mind' (в + prepositional), and помечтать is a prefixed infinitive 'to dream a little' or 'to daydream'. It continues the same elliptical sequence of things the speaker merely wanted to do in his head.
segments.p2-9-g352: А introduces a contrast 'but/while'. На деле literally 'on the matter' = 'in practice/in reality'. Мне надо is an impersonal-modal construction: dative мне + надо = 'I need' or 'it is necessary for me', implying an omitted infinitive that follows.
segments.p2-9-g353: Знаешь is the conversational 2nd-person present of знать used as a discourse particle 'you know'. Чего is a colloquial exclamatory particle here, completing the phrase 'you know what' and introducing a blunt statement; together they introduce what follows.
segments.p2-9-g354: Чтоб is the subordinating conjunction (colloquial form of чтобы) used to express the speaker's wish or purpose here ('that'/'so that' in the sense of 'that you should...'). Вы is the 2nd-person plural/formal pronoun. Провалились is the past plural form of the perfective провалиться used after чтоб to express a wished-for outcome — 'that you would fail/be ruined/go to hell' (a strong hostile wish).
segments.p2-9-g355: Вот is an emphatic particle here ('that's it' / 'that's what'), and чего is a matching exclamatory particle reinforcing the bluntness of the preceding wish — roughly: 'that's what!'.
segments.p2-9-g356: This is an impersonal construction: Мне (dative) + надо (modal 'need') + спокойствия (genitive singular of спокойствие) names what is needed. Literally 'To me is needed calm' = 'I need calm/peace.'
segments.p2-9-g357: Да is a colloquial, emphatic particle here ('why/indeed' / 'ah, but'—used to stress the following point). Я is the subject. За то literally 'for that' (за + demonstrative то) meaning 'in favor of that/for the sake of that' and sets up the clause that follows.
segments.p2-9-g358: Чтоб introduces the desired outcome 'that...'. Меня is the accusative 'me' as the object of беспокоили in a passive-style wish ('that they not bother me'). Не negates it. Беспокоили is 3rd-person plural past/plural-form used after чтоб to express the wish 'that (people) would not bother me' (the plural verb refers to an implied they/people).
segments.p2-9-g359: Весь свет means 'the whole world' or 'everyone' (весь + свет). Сейчас же is an emphatic temporal phrase 'right now/this very instant' (сейчас = now; же adds immediacy). За копейку literally 'for a kopeck' (за indicating price), and продам is 1st-person singular future perfective 'I will sell' (used hyperbolically here: 'I'd sell the whole world...'). The verb at the end completes the rhetorical clause.
segments.p2-9-g360: Свету is the dative singular of свет used idiomatically (dative + infinitive) to name the thing that 'suffers' the action — here 'the world' as the object of the wish/curse. The particle ли is enclitic and marks the clause as interrogative/rhetorical, and провалиться is the perfective infinitive 'to fall through/go to ruin', used here in an exclamatory/rhetorical sense ('let the world perish').
segments.p2-9-g361: This is an alternative (или) clause with разговорное emphasis вот ('well/now'). Мне is dative indicating the beneficiary ('for me' / 'as for me'). Чаю here is the colloquial/old form meaning 'tea' in a negative context (genitive-like), so the phrase means 'for me to drink tea (or not)'; не + пить is the infinitive negated ('not to drink').
segments.p2-9-g362: Я (I) is the subject; скажу is the first-person singular future of the perfective сказать, meaning 'I will say' or 'I'll say' — it introduces what the speaker would declare.
segments.p2-9-g363: Что introduces an indirect statement ('that ...'); свету провалиться repeats the dative + infinitive idiom ('let the world perish'). The infinitive functions as the predication within the subordinate clause rather than a finite verb form.
segments.p2-9-g364: А contrasts the second part with the first. Чтоб (colloquial shortened чтобы) introduces desired result/condition ('so that' / 'provided that') and is followed by the infinitive пить; мне is dative marking the beneficiary ('for me'); чай (accusative/nominal) is the object 'tea'; всегда is an adverb 'always'. The whole segment expresses the condition under which the speaker would accept the first clause.
segments.p2-9-g365: Знала is the past tense form of знать; here it functions as the predicate in a direct question ('did [you] know'). The enclitic ль marks the phrase as interrogative (whether/if), ты is the 2nd-person singular subject, and это is the demonstrative 'this/that' as the object.
segments.p2-9-g366: Или нет is a brief alternative closing to the question: 'or not?' — или is the coordinating 'or', нет is the negative response particle 'no'/'not'.
segments.p2-9-g367: Ну is an interjection often translated 'well' and used to introduce or soften what follows.
segments.p2-9-g368: А is a contrastive conjunction ('and/but/as for'); я is the pronoun 'I'; вот is a particle adding emphasis ('as for me' / 'indeed'); знаю is the present first-person singular of знать ('I know'). Together they mean 'but I know' or 'as for me, I know'.
segments.p2-9-g369: Что introduces the content of the speaker's knowledge ('that ...'); я repeats the subject for emphasis ('I'); мерзавец is a noun 'scoundrel/wretch' serving as a predicative complement.
segments.p2-9-g370: Подлец is another predicative noun in a list of self‑accusations — 'a cad' or 'villain' — coordinated by simple noun juxtaposition.
segments.p2-9-g371: Себялюбец is a compound noun meaning 'self‑lover' or 'egoist', continuing the series of self‑epithets; its position in the list reinforces the speaker's self‑reproach.
segments.p2-9-g372: Лентяй is the final noun in the list meaning 'idler' or 'sluggard', completing the catalogue of reprehensible traits the speaker ascribes to himself.
segments.p2-9-g440: Ведь is a discourse particle (‘after all’) that gives emphasis. Человек is an indefinite subject (‘a person’). Раз в жизни is an adverbial time phrase meaning ‘once in a lifetime’ (раз = once) and sits between subject and predicate. Только так (= ‘only in that way’) modifies the verb. Высказываться is the imperfective reflexive verb in 3rd person singular present, here used generically: ‘expresses himself / speaks out’.
segments.p2-9-g441: Да и то is a colloquial cluster meaning ‘and even that / and even then’ (да as old-fashioned conjunction + и то for emphasis). В + prepositional (истерике) indicates the state in which that expression occurred: ‘in a hysteria / in a hysterical outburst.’ The exclamatory tone is marked by the short clause and exclamation mark.
segments.p2-9-g444: Чего is the colloquial/genitive interrogative form of что used here for ‘what’ (often idiomatic). Ж is a particle adding emphasis (‘indeed/after all’). Тебе is dative ‘to you’ (indicating the person concerned), and ещё here means ‘more / else’ — together forming the short rhetorical question ‘What else (do you want)?’.
segments.p2-9-g445: Чего ж ты ещё is a colloquial, emphatic opening to a reproachful question: Чего (‘what/why’) + ж (emphatic particle) + ты (you) + ещё (‘still’). The phrase roughly means ‘Why are you still...’ with Чего used idiomatically rather than literally.
segments.p2-9-g446: После всего этого is a prepositional adverbial phrase: после (‘after’) + всего этого (‘all this’ in genitive) indicating the preceding actions or events — ‘after all this.’
segments.p2-9-g447: Торчишь is 2nd person singular present of торчать used colloquially (‘to loiter/stand about/loom’). Передо мной is a fixed locative expression meaning ‘in front of me / before me’ (передо is a short form of перед used before pronouns), with мной in the instrumental showing the person in front of whom the action occurs.
segments.p2-9-g448: Мучаешь меня is simple present 2nd person singular (мучить) with меня as the accusative object: ‘you torment / you’re tormenting me.’ It continues the pile-up of reproaches directed at the addressee.
segments.p2-9-g449: Не уходишь? is a negative interrogative in present tense (не + уходишь), literally ‘you do not leave?’ — an incredulous question equivalent to ‘why don’t you go?’ or ‘aren’t you going away?’ The short negative question increases the reproachful tone.
segments.p2-9-g450: Но = ‘but’ introduces contrast. Тут вдруг is a pair of adverbs meaning ‘then/at that moment’ (тут) and ‘suddenly’ (вдруг) and together set the timing. Случилось is the short past neuter form of случиться (‘occur/happen’) and is used impersonally; странное обстоятельство is the nominative neuter subject that follows the verb (inverted order): ‘a strange circumstance occurred.’
segments.p2-9-g373: Main clause: Я (subject) + дрожал (past tense, masculine singular of 'дрожать') describes a state; вот is a discourse particle adding emphasis ('you see' / 'indeed'); 'эти три дня' is a duration expression in the accusative meaning 'for those three days'; 'от страха' is a prepositional phrase giving the cause ('from/because of fear').
segments.p2-9-g374: Subordinate clause introduced by the complementizer что ('that') giving the content of the fear. Придешь is 2nd person singular future indicative (from прийти), literal 'you will come' — in English often rendered backshifted to 'you would come' because the fearing is in the past.
segments.p2-9-g375: А is a connective/discourse particle (here a mild conversational 'and/well'); знаешь is 2nd person singular present of знать used as a rhetorical question 'do you know'. The comma marks the pause before the object clause.
segments.p2-9-g376: That-clause functioning as the direct object of 'знаешь'. 'Все эти три дня' is a time-frame ('throughout/all those three days') placed before the verb for emphasis; меня is the affected person (accusative 'me'); особенно modifies беспокоило ('especially worried/disturbed'). Беспокоило is past neuter/impersonal form of беспокоить used with меня as the person disturbed.
segments.p2-9-g377: А то is a discourse marker meaning roughly 'namely' or 'but that' introducing the specific thing that worried him; it points forward to the explanatory clauses.
segments.p2-9-g378: Subordinate clause: что introduces the content. Вот and тогда add emphasis/temporal setting ('then, at that time'); представился is past reflexive of представить/представиться meaning 'presented myself / appeared (to you) as' and takes the instrumental (героем) for the role/identity ('as a hero such as that'); перед тобой = 'in your presence / before you.'
segments.p2-9-g379: Second coordinate clause connected by а ('and/yet'): тут вот ты вдруг увидишь меня is future-tense (увидишь) 'you will suddenly see me' with тут/вот/dруг вдруг adding immediacy/suddenness; 'в этом рваном халатишке' is locative/in-phrase identifying the shabby garment (халатишке is diminutive/derogatory, рваном = tattered).
segments.p2-9-g380: Appositive adjectival/noun phrase modifying 'меня' in the preceding clause: нищего is masculine animate accusative (forms look like genitive) and corresponds to 'poor/pauper' describing his state.
segments.p2-9-g381: Another appositive adjective in the same case as 'нищего' — гадкого describes personal quality ('nasty/disgusting').
segments.p2-9-g382: Main clause: Я сказал (past tense) with тебе as the dative indirect object ('I told you'); давеча is an adverb meaning 'recently / the other day.'
segments.p2-9-g383: Complement clause: что introduces 'I am not ashamed of my poverty.' Стыжусь is 1st person singular present of стыдиться/стыдиться-like verb; свою/своей with бедности is genitive because стыдиться takes the genitive ('ashamed of').
segments.p2-9-g384: So/therefore phrase: 'так' ('so') + imperative 'знай' (2nd person singular imperative 'know this') with emphatic particle 'же' — a forceful marker drawing attention to the following correction.
segments.p2-9-g385: Short clause introduced by что: 'that I am ashamed.' Here стыжусь is repeated for emphasis (1st person present).
segments.p2-9-g386: Comparative/emphatic phrase: 'больше всего' literally 'more than all' = 'most of all,' modifying the repeated стыжусь; the phrase intensifies the following predicate.
segments.p2-9-g387: Another intensifier 'пуще всего' (somewhat archaic/comparative) meaning 'more than anything' or 'above all' and боюсь is 1st person present 'I fear/am afraid.'
segments.p2-9-g388: Пуще того = 'more than that / furthermore' — a linking phrase that escalates the thought to the hypothetical clause that follows.
segments.p2-9-g389: Conditional (counterfactual) clause: 'если б' (если + particle б) marks a hypothetical contrary-to-fact condition; 'я воровал' uses the past imperfective to express the hypothetical action 'if I were to steal' (English uses 'if I stole' or 'if I were to steal').
segments.p2-9-g390: Causal connector 'потому что' = 'because'; 'я тщеславен так' = 'I am so vain' with 'так' as an intensifier ('so/that much').
segments.p2-9-g391: Comparative simile: 'как будто' = 'as if'; 'с меня кожу содрали' literally 'they stripped the skin off me' — с + genitive (меня) indicates removal from the person, and содрали is perfective past 'stripped off,' used figuratively to express extreme humiliation or pain.
segments.p2-9-g392: Final clause with experiencer в dative: 'и мне' ('and to me'), уж is an emphatic particle ('already/indeed'), 'от одного воздуха' ('from the mere air' = even the slightest breeze) and больно is an impersonal predicate meaning 'it hurts' or 'it is painful (to me).' Together: 'even the air alone already hurts me.'
segments.p2-9-g494: This is an impersonal/elliptical clause: the dative pronoun мне marks the experiencer/recipient, and the verb дают is present-tense plural (subject omitted, understood as 'they' or 'people') with the negator не before it. Together (мне не дают) idiomatically means 'I am not allowed' or 'they won't give/allow me.'
segments.p2-9-g497: Я is the subject; не negates the modal verb могу (1st person singular present of мочь, 'to be able'). The infinitive быть follows to express ability/possibility ('to be'). The clause is elliptical (the complement adjective is omitted here and supplied by the next line), so the structure is 'I am not able to be (X).' Word order is neutral, with the pronoun before the verb.
segments.p2-9-g500: Добрым is the adjective добрый in the instrumental singular (masculine), which is the normal case used after быть to indicate the quality someone 'is' (быть добрым = to be kind). Here it appears alone as an exclamation/complement to the previous elliptical clause ('I can't be kind!').
segments.p2-9-g501: Едва modifies the past perfective проговорил and here means 'scarcely' or 'hardly'; with the past tense it conveys that almost no time had passed after uttering the words. The inverted order проговорил я (verb before pronoun) emphasizes the action; together it reads 'I had scarcely uttered (it).'
segments.p2-9-g502: Затем is an adverb meaning 'then/afterwards.' Дошел is past masculine singular of дойти ('to reach/come up to') and governs the prepositional phrase до дивана ('up to the sofa'), indicating movement toward that object. The phrase means 'then I reached/went up to the sofa.'
segments.p2-9-g503: Упал is past tense 'fell' (physically). На него ('onto it') uses the preposition на plus the accusative pronoun него referring back to the sofa. Ничком is an adverb meaning 'face down' or 'prostrate.' И links the actions; четверть часа is a duration expression ('a quarter of an hour') with часа in the genitive after the numeral. Рыдал is past 'sobbed/wailed.' В настоящей истерике uses в + prepositional to mean 'in a real/genuine hysterical fit.'
segments.p2-9-g393: A cluster of particles and pronouns forming a surprised question: 'Да неужели ж' is an emphatic, rhetorical opening (expressing incredulity). 'Ты' is the nominative you; 'даже и теперь еще' stacks intensifiers ('even', 'also', 'now', 'still') to stress time and surprise; the clause ends with the perfective-past participle 'догадалась' used here as a past-polite perfect meaning 'have guessed' (female speaker form), negated by 'не'.
segments.p2-9-g394: 'что' introduces the content clause (that-clause). 'Я' is the subject; 'тебе' is the dative object 'to you'; 'никогда не прощу' is a future-tense idiomatic negation: 'I will never forgive' (хроничесic present with future force — first-person singular of 'простить'). 'Того' is a genitive demonstrative referring forward to the thing for which he won't forgive her.
segments.p2-9-g395: Another that-clause (explanatory 'что'): 'ты застала меня' — perfective past 'застать' in the 2nd person singular past meaning 'you caught/found me (in the act)'; 'в этом халатишке' is a locative prepositional phrase 'in this little/poor dressing-gown' (diminutive/derogatory). This entire clause is the object of 'не прощу того'.
segments.p2-9-g396: 'Когда' introduces a temporal subordinate clause 'when I was ...'; 'я бросался' is imperfective past reflexive 'бросаться' in 1st person singular past ('I was throwing myself/rushing'), used to describe the action ongoing at that moment when she caught him.
segments.p2-9-g397: A simile introduced by 'как' ('like/as'); 'злая' is an adjective 'angry/spiteful' modifying the diminutive pejorative 'собачонка' ('little dog/cur'), producing the image 'like an angry little dog.'
segments.p2-9-g398: 'На' + accusative names the object of the lunge: 'на Аполлона' — 'at Apollon'; here 'Аполлона' is a proper name in accusative, referring to a man (not the god) and 'на' takes the meaning 'toward/at' with verbs of attack.
segments.p2-9-g399: 'Воскреситель' is a nominalizing noun used ironically ('the resurrector' or 'revived one'); the clitic particle '-то' attaches for emphasis/contrast (colloquial/spoken emphasis marker), drawing attention to this label.
segments.p2-9-g400: Apposition: 'бывший-то герой' — 'the former — indeed — hero', where 'бывший' ('former') modifies 'герой' and the particle '-то' again adds contrastive emphasis; the phrase stresses ironic loss of heroic status.
segments.p2-9-g401: 'бросается' is the present tense, reflexive form of 'бросать(ся)' meaning 'rushes at' or 'throws himself' (here habitual/presentive narration: the action is being depicted as happening now or characteristically).
segments.p2-9-g402: 'как паршивая' begins the simile 'like a mangy (one)': 'паршивая' is a derogatory adjective ('mangy/wretched') used predicatively after 'как' to heighten contempt.
segments.p2-9-g403: 'лохматая шавка' continues the simile: 'лохматая' ('shaggy') modifies 'шавка' (pejorative 'yappy dog/cur'), giving the image 'a mangy, shaggy cur.'
segments.p2-9-g404: 'на своего лакея' is the prepositional object of 'бросается' — 'at his own footman'; 'своего' is the reflexive possessive matching the subject, and 'лакея' is accusative 'footman' (a social inferior).
segments.p2-9-g405: 'А тот смеется над ним!' is a contrasting clause: 'а' = 'but/and', 'тот' = 'the one/the latter' (refers to the footman), 'смеется' is present 'laughs', and 'над ним' uses 'над' with instrumental 'ним' to mean 'to mock/at him.'
segments.p2-9-g406: 'И слез давешних' — 'And the tears of the other day'; 'слез' is the genitive plural of 'слеза' (here 'tears' emotional), and 'давешних' is an adjective meaning 'of the other day' or 'recent', qualifying those tears.
segments.p2-9-g407: 'которых перед тобой я' is a relative clause opener: 'which, in your presence, I ...' — 'которых' is genitive plural referring back to 'слез'; 'перед тобой' = 'in front of you/before you' (prepositional phrase with 'тобой' in instrumental), and 'я' is the subject pronoun.
segments.p2-9-g408: 'как пристыженная баба' is a simile: 'like a woman made ashamed' — 'пристаженная/пристаженная' is a short participial adjective meaning 'shamed' (passive sense/feeling), and 'баба' is a colloquial 'woman' (pejorative tone), together portraying shameful, feminine behavior.
segments.p2-9-g409: 'не мог удержать' is the negated past ability: 'I could not hold back' — 'не' negates, 'мог' is past tense of 'мочь' (imperfective 'was able'), and 'удержать' (perfective infinitive) 'to restrain/hold back' completes the construction.
segments.p2-9-g410: Main clause conclusion: 'никогда тебе не прощу!' — 'I will never forgive you!' — 'никогда' (never), 'тебе' (to you, dative), negative 'не' plus future-meaning verb 'прощу' (1st person singular of 'простить').
segments.p2-9-g411: 'И того' begins with 'И' ('and') followed by 'того' (genitive demonstrative 'of that/that one'), pointing forward to the matter being named in the subordinate clause; it's the object of the later 'не прощу'.
segments.p2-9-g412: 'в чем теперь тебе признаюсь' is a relative/content clause 'in which/what I now confess to you': 'в чем' = 'in which/what', 'теперь' = 'now', 'тебе' dative 'to you', and 'признаюсь' is 1st person singular future/presentive of 'признаться' meaning 'I will/must confess' (here presentive about an imminent confession).
segments.p2-9-g413: 'тоже никогда тебе не прощу!' repeats and emphasizes the refusal to forgive: 'тоже' ('also/too') links this grievance to the previous ones; the standard negated future 'никогда ... не прощу' again states 'I will never forgive you.'
segments.p2-9-g531: This whole short clause is an emphatic, rhetorical question. да is a colloquial introductory particle (here: a mild exclamation/’well’). неужели is a rhetorical adverb meaning ‘really?/could it be that…?’; ж is a reinforcing particle adding emphasis. Я is the explicit 1st-person subject; тогда places the action in that earlier time; ей is the dative object (‘to/for her’); позавидовал is the past tense, perfective verb (masculine singular), ‘envied’ — together forming ‘Did I really envy her then?’
segments.p2-9-g532: Не знаю is a short, idiomatic comment clause: the negation не + 1st-person present знаю of знать = ‘I don't know’. It stands alone as a spontaneous remark rather than part of a subordinate clause.
segments.p2-9-g533: До сих пор is a fixed temporal phrase meaning ‘until now/so far’ (до + demonstrative сих + noun пор ‘time’). Ещё (‘still/yet’) intensifies continuity; не могу is the present negative of мочь (‘I cannot’); решить is the infinitive ‘to decide/resolve’, completing the modal ‘cannot decide’. Word order is standard: time phrase → adverb → modal → infinitive.
segments.p2-9-g534: А тогда is a brief contrastive marker: а (‘but/and’) shifts to a contrasting time reference, then тогда (‘then/in that case’) points to the earlier period just mentioned. The comma marks the pause before the explanatory clause.
segments.p2-9-g535: Конечно is an adverbial modal ‘of course’ qualifying the remark that follows; set off by commas it signals the speaker's confidence about the general point.
segments.p2-9-g536: Ещё меньше modifies the capacity expressed by мог: ещё (‘even/more’) + меньше (comparative ‘less’) yields ‘even less’. Мог is past tense, masculine singular of мочь (‘could’), with an implicit subject я. Это is a demonstrative pronoun ‘this/that’ referring to the preceding idea; понять is an infinitive ‘understand’. Together: ‘I could understand that even less.’
segments.p2-9-g537: Чем is the comparative particle ‘than’ linking the degree expressed earlier to the standard of comparison; теперь (‘now’) gives the time used for comparison. The structure means ‘…less than now.’
segments.p2-9-g538: The sentence begins with the preposition без (without) governing the genitive, so власти and тиранства are genitives tied by и (and) to mean 'without power and (without) tyranny/despotism.' The phrase над кем-нибудь uses над + the pronoun кем with the particle-нибудь to form an indefinite instrumental phrase 'over someone/anyone.' The main clause is я ... не могу прожить: the subject я (I) is followed by the emphatic particle ведь, then the negated modal не могу (cannot, present tense of мочь) plus the infinitive прожить (to live/exist), so the construction expresses inability to live/exist without the preceding condition.
segments.p2-9-g541: Но is a coordinating conjunction here used alone as a one-word sentence to signal contrast or a sudden reservation (a common rhetorical device in Russian narrative). It functions as a discourse connector: 'but,' 'however.'
segments.p2-9-g451: Я до того привык — 'Я' is the subject; 'до того' is an adverbial intensifier meaning 'to such an extent' or 'so much'; 'привык' is the past-tense, masculine perfective verb 'became accustomed'. The following series of infinitives (думать и воображать ... и представлять) are the complements of привык, and 'себе' is the dative reflexive used with представлять ('to picture to oneself'). 'по книжке' means 'according to the book / by the book' and modifies how he thought/imaged.
segments.p2-9-g452: 'как' introduces a comparative clause ('just as'); 'сам' is emphatic/reflexive 'myself'; 'еще прежде' is a temporal adverbial phrase 'earlier / even before'; 'в мечтах' is prepositional 'in (my) dreams' (мечтах in plural is the normal form); 'сочинил' is past tense perfective 'composed / invented', placed at the end in typical Russian clause order.
segments.p2-9-g453: 'что' introduces the result clause ('that...'); 'даже' is an intensifier 'even'; 'сразу' means 'immediately'; 'и не понял' is the negated past ('and did not understand') — here 'и' is a small connective often reinforcing the negation together with 'даже'; 'тогда' 'then'; 'этого странного обстоятельства' is a genitive object 'that strange circumstance' (это → этого because of genitive required after понять in sense 'to realize/understand [of] / about').
segments.p2-9-g454: 'А' is a contrasting conjunction often rendered 'and' or 'but' introducing the statement; 'случилось' is impersonal/past 'happened'; 'вот' is an emphatic particle drawing attention; 'что' introduces the explanatory clause that follows — the whole phrase functions as 'and what happened was this:'.
segments.p2-9-g455: Proper name 'Лиза' in the nominative: the subject of the following participial phrase and main clause.
segments.p2-9-g456: 'оскорбленная и раздавленная мною' is a participial phrase modifying Лиза: both are past passive participles (оскорбленная 'offended/humiliated', раздавленная 'crushed/overwhelmed') and 'мною' is the instrumental agent 'by me' (archaic/formal instrumental pronoun instead of 'мной').
segments.p2-9-g457: 'поняла' is the past tense, feminine of 'понять' ('she understood'); 'гораздо' is an adverb 'much / far' that intensifies the comparative 'больше' ('more'), so the phrase means 'understood far more.'
segments.p2-9-g458: 'чем' introduces the comparative clause ('than'); 'я' is the subject; 'воображал' is past tense masculine of 'воображать' ('I had imagined'); 'себе' is the dative reflexive used with воображать: 'imagined to myself' / 'I had imagined'.
segments.p2-9-g459: 'Она поняла' past tense 'she understood'; 'из всего этого' is a prepositional phrase 'from all this' (из + genitive); 'то' is a demonstrative pronoun 'that' introducing the content clause that follows (object of поняла).
segments.p2-9-g460: 'что' begins the content clause: 'a woman will always first of all understand'; 'женщина' is the generic subject; 'всегда' 'always'; 'прежде всего' is an idiom meaning 'first of all' (here split into прежде + всего); 'поймет' is future simple (perfective) third person singular: 'will understand'.
segments.p2-9-g461: 'если' introduces the conditional 'if'; 'искренно' is an adverb 'sincerely'; 'любит' is present-tense third person singular 'loves' — the subject is understood to be 'женщина' from the previous clause.
segments.p2-9-g462: 'а именно:' is a formulaic phrase 'namely' or 'that is to say', where 'а' is a connective and 'именно' pinpoints the forthcoming clarification.
segments.p2-9-g463: 'что' introduces the explanatory clause: 'that I myself am unhappy.' 'я' is subject; 'сам' is emphatic ('myself' / 'I personally'); 'несчастлив' is a short-form adjective used predicatively meaning 'unhappy' (stative, not a full adjectival phrase).
segments.p2-9-g464: 'Испуганное и оскорбленное чувство' is a noun phrase: 'чувство' (feeling) modified by two past participles/adjectives (испуганное 'frightened', оскорбленное 'offended'); 'сменилось' is the neuter past reflexive 'changed / was replaced'; the phrase 'на лице ее' (на + locative/accusative with possessive 'ее') indicates location 'on her face'; 'сначала' 'at first'; 'горестным изумлением' is in the instrumental case, which Russian uses after смениться to indicate what it changed into ('into a sorrowful astonishment').
segments.p2-9-g481: Она is the personal subject ('she'). вдруг is an adverb of suddenness modifying вскочила, which is the past‑tense, perfective verb (вскочить) meaning 'to spring/jump up'. со стула uses the preposition со (variant of с used before the consonant cluster) + genitive стула to mean 'from the chair'. в каком-то неудержимом порыве is a prepositional phrase (в + prepositional) with the indefinite modifier каком‑то ('in some/one of those') and the adjective неудержимом agreeing with порыве, giving the sense 'in some irresistible impulse'. The trailing и links this clause to the following participial/gerund phrase.
segments.p2-9-g482: вся here is an intensifying form of весь used adverbially with the gerund стремясь; the gerund (деепричастие) стремясь from стремиться expresses attendant action 'hurrying/striving toward'. ко мне is dative (to/toward me). Together the phrase describes her attitude/manner as she sprang up.
segments.p2-9-g483: но 'but' introduces contrast. все ещё (все + ещё) is an adverbial intensifier meaning 'still'. робея is a present gerund (деепричастие) from робеть 'being timid/fearing', describing simultaneous hesitation. и не смея contains the negative particle не + the archaic/elliptical gerund смея (from сметь) meaning 'not daring'; with the infinitive сойти (perfective infinitive 'to step down/leave') and the prepositional phrase с места ('from her spot/seat'), it means she did not dare to get up from her place.
segments.p2-9-g484: протянула is past tense, perfective, feminine singular of протянуть 'to stretch out/hold out'; ко мне is dative 'toward me'; руки is the accusative plural object 'hands/arms'. The simple S‑V‑O structure states the final action: she reached out her hands to me.
segments.p2-9-g487: Тут is an adverb meaning 'then/at that moment' (not strictly spatial). сердце is the nominative subject 'heart' (here emotional core). и functions as an intensifier 'even/too' before the phrase во мне ('in me' — во is variant of в used before certain consonants). перевернулось is a reflexive past form (neuter singular impersonal use) of перевернуться 'to turn over' used figuratively: 'my heart turned over.' The order places emphasis on the sudden internal feeling.
segments.p2-9-g488: Тогда sets the temporal point 'then/at that moment'. она is the subject; вдруг is the suddenness adverb; бросилась is the past reflexive perfective verb броситься 'to rush, throw oneself' and takes ко мне (dative) meaning 'toward me/at me'. The clause describes an abrupt physical movement.
segments.p2-9-g489: обхватила is past‑tense perfective of обхватить 'to encircle/embrace' and takes мою шею in the accusative 'my neck' as its object; руками is an instrumental plural that specifies the means ('with her hands'). и links the two coordinated verbs; заплакала is the perfective past of заплакать 'to begin/ burst into crying'. The sequence shows she embraced him and then began to cry.
segments.p2-9-g490: Я is the subject 'I'. тоже 'too/also' marks that the narrator is affected as well. не выдержал is the past tense of выдержать with negation meaning 'could not hold out/endure' (i.e. 'couldn't restrain myself'). и links to the next action; зарыдал is past from зарыдать 'to burst into sobs'. так here points forward to the manner/degree ('in such a way/as such').
segments.p2-9-g491: как introduces a comparative clause 'as/like'. никогда ещё со мной не бывало is an idiomatic impersonal construction meaning 'had never happened to me before' — никогда (never) + ещё (yet/still, here: 'ever before') intensify the negation; со мной ('with me') marks the experiencer; не бывало is the impersonal past of бывать meaning 'used to happen' negated to mean 'had not occurred'. Together it yields 'as I had never experienced before.'
segments.p2-9-g561: Глаза мои is an inverted noun + possessive ('my eyes') for emphasis (normal order would be мои глаза). Блеснули is past tense, plural, perfective (they flashed/sparkled). Страстью is instrumental case meaning 'with passion' and indicates the manner of the flashing.
segments.p2-9-g562: И links the clauses. Я is the nominative subject 'I'. Крепко is an adverb modifying стиснул and means 'firmly/tightly'. Стиснул is past tense, masculine singular, perfective (I squeezed/clinched) and takes a direct object. Ее before руки is a possessive/object pronoun 'her', and руки is accusative plural (form identical to nominative) meaning 'hands'.
segments.p2-9-g563: Как at the start is exclamatory 'how'. Я ненавидел ее is a straightforward past imperfective clause 'I hated her' with ее as the direct object. The second как begins another exclamation introducing an impersonal construction: меня влекло к ней literally 'it drew me toward her' — влекло is past neuter (impersonal/psychological construction) and меня is the affected accusative; к ней is the prepositional object 'to/toward her'. В эту минуту is a time phrase 'at this moment'.
segments.p2-9-g564: Одно чувство is the nominative subject ('one feeling'); одно is a neuter numeral/adjective agreeing with чувство. Усиливало is past tense, neuter singular, imperfective ('intensified'/'was intensifying') agreeing with the neuter subject. Другое is the direct object in accusative (neuter form identical to nominative) meaning 'another (feeling)'.
segments.p2-9-g565: Это is a demonstrative pronoun 'this/it' serving as the subject. Походило is past tense, neuter (это + verb) of походить used with на + accusative to mean 'resembled/was like'. Чуть не is a fixed collocation meaning 'almost' or 'nearly'. На introduces the complement in accusative (мщение) and with походить gives 'like/resembling'. Мщение is the accusative noun 'revenge/vengeance'.
segments.p2-9-g568: На лице ее is a locative prepositional phrase ('on her face') placed before the verb; ее is the possessive pronoun 'her' placed after the noun. Изобразилось is a short-form reflexive past tense verb (neuter singular) used impersonally here — the grammatical agreement with the actual subject (недоумение, a neuter noun) makes the verb appear in neuter past, so the clause literally reads 'on her face there was depicted/assumed at first a perplexity.' Сначала is a temporal adverb 'at first' modifying the verb; как будто introduces a simile 'as if/as though' connecting недоумение as the content of the expression.
segments.p2-9-g569: This is an elliptical continuation of the previous clause supplying another shade of the expression: как будто 'as if' + даже 'even' emphasizes the surprising additional quality, страх functions as the noun 'fear'. Grammatically there is no new verb; the phrase modifies the same predicate (изобразилось).
segments.p2-9-g570: Но is the contrasting conjunction 'but'; только is the restrictive adverb 'only/just' qualifying the duration; на мгновение is a preposition + accusative noun phrase meaning 'for a moment' (на + accusative expresses a short duration here). Together they limit the previously described expression to a very brief time.
segments.p2-9-g571: Она is the subject 'she'. Обняла is a past-tense, perfective verb in the feminine singular form agreeing with она, indicating a completed action 'embraced'. Вoсторженно и горячо are adverbial modifiers of manner placed before the verb ('enthusiastically and ardently'); и coordinates the two adverbs. Меня is the accusative object 'me'. Word order (Subject – adverbials – Verb – Object) is natural and emphasizes the manner of the embrace.
segments.p2-9-g414: Да here is a short exclamatory particle — an emphatic 'yes' or 'well' that opens the outburst; it does not introduce a question and simply marks affirmation/emphasis.
segments.p2-9-g415: Ты is the direct vocative/you (singular) addressed with strong stress; the dash and pause heighten the direct address.
segments.p2-9-g416: Одна ты ('only you') places the restrictive adjective before the pronoun for emphasis. The clause uses the infinitive + modal construction ответить должна — the infinitive 'ответить' (to answer/answer for) comes before the modal/adjectival 'должна' (ought/must), a common Russian order that yields 'you must answer for all this'. 'За все это' assigns the object/ground 'for all this' (за + accusative/genitive sense).
segments.p2-9-g417: Потому что introduces the explanatory clause 'because ...'. Ты так подвернулась uses подвернуться in the past (feminine form) meaning 'to present oneself / to turn up / to happen to show up' (colloquial), so 'you happened to turn up' — так intensifies the accidental quality.
segments.p2-9-g418: Another reason-clause with потому что; the predicate is the nominative noun мерзавец ('a scoundrel/wretch') predicated of 'я' — simple self-condemnation in the nominative.
segments.p2-9-g419: Потому что + clause again. Самый + adjective forms a superlative 'the very/most ...' — 'я самый гадкий' = 'I am the vilest'.
segments.p2-9-g420: Elliptical continuation of the superlative list: 'самый смешной' repeats the same structure with the subject (я) omitted for stylistic brevity; Самый forms the superlative.
segments.p2-9-g421: Again a superlative 'самый' + adjective 'мелочной' (here an older/colloquial adjectival ending) meaning 'most petty'; the unusual ending is stylistic but functions grammatically as the adjective in a superlative series.
segments.p2-9-g422: Continuation of the self‑listing with 'самый глупый' = 'the most stupid/foolish'; the superlative adjective remains in parallel with the previous items.
segments.p2-9-g423: Самый завистливый 'the most envious' followed by a restrictive phrase 'из всех на земле червяков' — 'of all the worms on earth' — where 'из всех' takes the genitive plural червяков and 'на земле' ('on earth') localizes the set.
segments.p2-9-g424: Которые introduces a relative clause modifying 'червяков'. Вовсе не лучше меня is a negated comparative meaning 'by no means better than me' — вовсе intensifies the negation 'не'.
segments.p2-9-g425: Но ('but') marks contrast; которые repeats the relative pronoun beginning another clause fragment continuing the description of those 'worms'.
segments.p2-9-g426: Черт знает отчего is an idiomatic expletive phrase ('the devil/God knows why') where 'черт' functions as an exclamation, 'знает' is literal 'knows', and 'отчего' = 'for what reason/why'.
segments.p2-9-g427: Никогда не конфузятся is a simple present-tense (3rd pl.) negative clause: 'they are never embarrassed' — конфузятся is a reflexive verb meaning 'be embarrassed' or 'be put to shame' and refers back to 'которые'.
segments.p2-9-g428: А вот introduces a sharp contrast ('but as for me' / 'whereas I...'). Я так всю жизнь буду получать щелчки от всякой гниды uses the future-periphrastic construction (буду + infinitive получать) to mean a habitual/future continual action: 'I will be getting little flicks/snaps from every low creature my whole life.' И это моя черта is a summative clause 'and that is my trait/characteristic.'
segments.p2-9-g429: Да какое мне дело до того is a colloquial rhetorical formula: 'Что какое мне дело до того' = 'What business is it of mine as to that.' До + genitive (того) expresses 'regarding/as to'. The whole phrase is a blunt dismissal.
segments.p2-9-g430: Что introduces the content clause explaining what he doesn't care about. Ты этого ничего не поймешь is a negative construction (ничего не + verb) meaning 'you will understand nothing of this' — поймешь is future 2nd-person singular.
segments.p2-9-g431: И какое is the opening of another rhetorical question; 'И' links this question to the previous remark and 'какое' begins the 'what ...' formula.
segments.p2-9-g432: Ну какое adds an exclamatory 'well, what' — 'ну' softens/heightens the incredulous tone and repeats the interrogative 'какое' for emphasis.
segments.p2-9-g433: Которое дело мне до тебя и до того repeats the core formula 'what business is it of mine' with two objects: 'до тебя' (regarding you) and 'до того' (regarding that). До + genitive marks the sphere of concern.
segments.p2-9-g434: Погибаешь ты там или нет? is a coordinate interrogative clause 'whether you perish there or not' with погибаешь present tense (used here to form the 'or not' alternative), ты as subject, and или нет marking the alternative 'or not.'
segments.p2-9-g435: Да понимаешь ли ты is a direct question 'do you understand' where ли is the enclitic interrogative particle. Да is a small emphatic particle at the start (roughly 'well'/'so') and ты is the addressed pronoun.
segments.p2-9-g436: Как я теперь begins the subordinate clause 'how I now ...' — как = 'how' (introducing the manner), я = 'I', теперь = 'now/at present'.
segments.p2-9-g437: Высказав тебе это is a perfective gerund (деепричастие) 'having expressed/ said this to you'; it denotes an action completed prior to the main verb and modifies the following clause.
segments.p2-9-g438: Тебя ненавидеть буду за то puts the object тебя before the infinitive ненавидеть for emphasis; буду (future auxiliary) + infinitive indicates 'I will hate you'. За то introduces the reason ('for the fact that...').
segments.p2-9-g439: Что ты тут была и слушала? is the content clause explaining the reason: 'that you were here and listened.' Была and слушала are past-tense feminine forms agreeing with ты (female addressee); тут = 'here/at this place/time.'
segments.p2-9-g465: "Когда же" is a temporal connector with the emphatic particle же, here meaning 'when (then)'; "я стал называть себя" uses стать + infinitive to mean 'I began to call myself' (стал = past 'began'); nouns after называть себя appear in the instrumental (подлецом, мерзавцем). The clause then continues with "и полились мои слезы," where полились is a past reflexive verb 'poured/flowed' and the unusual word order (verb before possessive subject мои) is stylistic inversion. The parenthesis "(я проговорил всю эту тираду со слезами)" is a simple past perfective statement: проговорил = 'I uttered/said' + direct object тираду; со слезами is instrumental indicating accompaniment.
segments.p2-9-g466: "все лицо ее" = 'her whole face' (все + noun for 'entire'); передернулось is a past reflexive verb meaning 'twitched/jerked' (middle voice) and is followed by an instrumental phrase "какой-то судорогой" ('with some kind of spasm'), where какой-то is an indefinite qualifier ('some / some kind of') and судорогой is the instrumental singular of судорога indicating the manner or cause.
segments.p2-9-g467: Она хотела было встать: хотела is the past tense 'wanted / meant to', and the particle было after a past-tense verb (хотела было) marks an attempted or intended action ('was about to'); встать is the infinitive 'to get up/stand up' functioning as the complement.
segments.p2-9-g468: остановить меня is the perfective infinitive 'to stop / to prevent' with the direct object меня (Accusative): it expresses the purpose or intended action complementary to хотела было встать (she meant/get up in order to stop me).
segments.p2-9-g469: когда же я кончил: когда (when) with the emphatic же, and кончил is the past perfective 'I finished / was done' — a temporal clause that sets the moment for what follows.
segments.p2-9-g470: она не на крики мои обратила внимание: the verb phrase обратить внимание на = 'to pay attention to'; here it is negated (не обратила внимание), and the object phrase на крики мои ('to my cries') is fronted for emphasis (word order inverted from the neutral обратила внимание на мои крики).
segments.p2-9-g471: Quoted exclamation: "Зачем ты здесь" — Зачем is the rhetorical 'why / what for', ты is the explicit subject 'you' (singular), здесь 'here'. The following repeated 'зачем не уходишь!' uses the same interrogative adverb but with the present-tense verb уходишь ('you are not leaving / you don't leave') — the subject ты is omitted the second time but understood from the first clause; the present here is exclamatory.
segments.p2-9-g472: "- а на то" continues the idea of what she attended to: а is a contrastive 'but/and', and на то is a pronominal prepositional phrase meaning 'to that / to the fact' (object of обратить внимание).
segments.p2-9-g473: что мне, introduces a subordinate clause with что = 'that'; мне is dative and marks the experiencer/affected person ('for me' / 'to me') in the impersonal construction that follows.
segments.p2-9-g474: должно быть is an idiomatic parenthetical modal phrase meaning 'it must be / probably' (должно expresses modality 'ought to / must', with быть as the infinitive), used here as 'I suppose / it must have been.'
segments.p2-9-g475: очень тяжело самому было все это выговорить: an impersonal construction where было + adjective (тяжело) expresses 'it was difficult'; очень modifies тяжело; самому (dative) emphasizes 'for me personally / myself'; the infinitive выговорить ('to say/utter') is the action that was difficult, and все это is its object ('to say all this').
segments.p2-9-g476: Да и забитая она была такая,: Да и is a colloquial connective meaning 'and/besides'; забитая is a past participle used predicatively here ('beaten-down, cowed'); она была такая = 'she was such (that...)' — the structure emphasizes her downtrodden state.
segments.p2-9-g477: бедная; is a short exclamatory predicate/adjective 'poor (thing)' referring to her — here an interjection of pity placed after the clause.
segments.p2-9-g478: она считала себя бесконечно ниже меня;: считала себя is 'she considered herself', бесконечно is an adverb 'infinitely/highly' intensifying the comparative ниже ('lower / beneath'), and меня is the comparison standard ('than me'). The comparative expresses social/moral inferiority: 'she regarded herself as far beneath me.'
segments.p2-9-g479: где ж ей было озлиться,: a rhetorical question introduced by где ж (where/then) with ж adding emphasis; ей is dative 'for her', было is the impersonal past 'was', and the infinitive озлиться 'to become angry' expresses the action she could hardly perform — 'how could she possibly get angry?'.
segments.p2-9-g480: обидеться?: a parallel infinitive 'to take offense / be offended' completing the rhetorical question — terse, rhetorical, and expecting the answer 'she couldn't.'
segments.p2-9-g518: Не is the sentential negation particle placed before the verb; знаю is 1st person singular present indicative of знать, so не знаю = "I don't know." The dash and pause mark an abrupt spoken fragment; the subject "I" is omitted because it is encoded in the verb ending.
segments.p2-9-g519: Но is the coordinating adversative particle "but." Мне is the dative pronoun meaning "to me" used here in an impersonal construction; было is past neuter of быть and functions as the past-marker in the impersonal clause; стыдно is an indeclinable predicative adjective/adverb meaning "shameful/ashamed," so вместе "мне было стыдно" = "I felt ashamed."
segments.p2-9-g520: Пришло here is the neuter past used impersonally with the dative мне to mean "occurred to me" ("came to my mind"). Тоже = "also/too." В + accusative взбудораженную мою голову is a slightly marked word order (adjective + possessive + noun) meaning "into my agitated head/mind;" голову is understood figuratively as "mind."
segments.p2-9-g521: Что introduces the subordinate content clause "that..." Роли is the plural nominative subject "roles." Ведь is an emphatic particle meaning "after all" or "you know." Теперь "now," окончательно "definitively/finally," and переменились is the past plural reflexive/perfective meaning "changed/been transformed," so the clause says the roles have now finally changed.
segments.p2-9-g522: Another что introduces a further specification: героиня теперь она places the pronoun она after the noun for identification/emphasis ("the heroine is now her/she"). This is an equative/identifying order used for contrast or emphasis.
segments.p2-9-g523: А introduces a contrast ("and/but"). Я is the nominative subject; the verb linking is omitted (colloquial equative style) and understood from context. Точно такое же is a fixed phrase meaning "exactly the same" (точно = exactly; такое же = such/the same), же is an emphatic particle. Униженное и раздавленное are past participles used adjectivally to modify создание (a neuter noun), yielding "the same humiliated and crushed creature."
segments.p2-9-g524: Каким here is a relative/comparative pronoun in the required case (instrumental/qualitative) linking to создание: "the creature such as she had been." Она была is past tense "she was." Передо мной is the prepositional phrase meaning "in my presence/before me" (передо is a variant of перед used before pronouns), and в ту ночь means "on that night." Together the clause means "as she had been before me on that night."
segments.p2-9-g525: Four-day time expression: четыре дня uses the genitive дня because numerals from 2–4 govern genitive singular; назад is the postposition meaning "ago/earlier," so четыре дня назад = "four days ago/four days earlier."
segments.p2-9-g528: И is simply "and." Всё это is the neutral object "all this." Ко мне пришло is the same impersonally phrased "came to me/occurred to me," with ко + dative. Ещё in this temporal position adds the sense "even/still/already" (emphasizing that it happened at that time), and в те минуты is a temporal adverbial "in those moments."
segments.p2-9-g529: Когда introduces the temporal subordinate clause "when." Я лежал is past imperfective "I was lying/laying," describing an ongoing posture. Ничком is an adverb meaning "face down/prostrate." На диване is the locative prepositional phrase "on the sofa/couch." The whole clause: "when I was lying face down on the sofa."
segments.p2-9-g530: Боже is the vocative or exclamatory form of бог used in direct address and interjection ("God!"); мой is the possessive pronoun "my," yielding the common exclamation Боже мой = "My God!" or "Oh my God!".
segments.p2-9-g544: но (contrastive conjunction) + ведь (emphatic particle) introduce a counterpoint: 'but after all'. Рассуждениями is the instrumental plural of рассуждение and expresses means ('by/through reasoning'). Ничего не объяснишь is a negative construction with the 2nd-person singular verb объяснишь (future/simple perfective sense here used as a general statement): literally 'you will explain nothing', i.e. 'you can't explain anything'.
segments.p2-9-g545: а is a connective contrasting the next clause with the previous one ('and/while' → here 'and/so'). Следственно is an adverb meaning 'consequently/therefore', summing up the result of the preceding clause.
segments.p2-9-g546: и is a simple coordinator ('and/so'). Рассуждать is the infinitive 'to reason/discuss'. Нечего is the impersonal particle meaning 'there is nothing (to ...)' when used with an infinitive, so 'there is nothing to reason about' / 'no point in reasoning'.
segments.p2-9-g547: Я is the nominative pronoun 'I', the subject of the sentence; the following comma marks a pause before a parenthetical adverb.
segments.p2-9-g548: однако is an adversative adverb 'however/nevertheless'. The enclitic particle ж adds emphasis ('indeed/after all'), making the phrase a marked concessive comment: 'I, however (indeed)...'.
segments.p2-9-g549: Преодолел себя is a reflexive past-tense perfective phrase meaning 'I overcame/forced myself' (преодолеть себя = to conquer one's reluctance). И coordinates the two past actions; приподнял (perfective past) + голову (accusative) means 'slightly raised (my) head' — both verbs are masculine singular past, describing successive acts by the same subject.
segments.p2-9-g550: Надобно было is an impersonal past construction expressing necessity: 'it was necessary'. Ж again adds emphasis. Когда-нибудь (когда + нибудь) is an indefinite adverb meaning 'sometime/ever'; поднять is the infinitive 'to raise' — together: 'it had to be raised sometime'.
segments.p2-9-g553: И вот is a discourse marker meaning roughly 'and so' / 'and now' / 'and behold', used to introduce the speaker's conclusion or result; it links what follows to the preceding narrative.
segments.p2-9-g554: я is the subject 'I'. До сих пор is an idiomatic temporal phrase ('up to this time' / 'to this day') formed from до ('until/up to'), сих (archaic genitive of 'this') and пор ('time/period'). Уверен is a predicative adjective 'sure/certain' agreeing with the subject (I am sure).
segments.p2-9-g555: что is the subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing the content clause. Именно is an intensifier 'exactly/precisely'. Потому is an adverb 'for that reason' — the three together mean 'that precisely because...'.
segments.p2-9-g556: Что begins the explanatory subordinate clause 'that'. Мне было стыдно is an impersonal construction where the dative мне marks the experiencer: literally 'to me it was shameful' → 'I was ashamed'. Смотреть на нее is the infinitive phrase 'to look at her' (на + pronoun for the object).
segments.p2-9-g557: В сердце моем is a locative phrase 'in my heart' (в + noun + archaic possessive моем). Вдруг тогда ('suddenly then') places the action in time. Зажглось и вспыхнуло are two neuter past perfective verbs agreeing with the subject чувство (omitted before the verbs but implied here): 'kindled and flashed/flare[d] up'. Другое чувство is the subject 'another feeling' following the verbs (inversion for style).
segments.p2-9-g560: Чувство is the noun 'feeling'. Господства is a genitive noun here meaning 'of domination/dominance' and denotes the quality of the feeling; и simply connects the two genitives. Обладания is another genitive noun 'of possession' (possession/mastery), so the whole phrase is 'a feeling of domination and possession'.
segments.p2-9-g504: Она is the subject pronoun 'she'. припала is the past tense, feminine singular form of припасть/припать (perfective/immediate-action verb) meaning 'pressed herself close' or 'sprang to', describing a completed sudden movement toward the speaker. ко мне is the preposition к in its euphonic variant ко (used before certain pronouns) plus the dative мне, meaning 'toward me'.
segments.p2-9-g505: обняла is the past tense, feminine singular of the perfective verb обнять 'to embrace/hug' with меня as its direct object (accusative). и links this action to the next; как бы is a two-word particle (как + бы) meaning 'as if' or softening the statement. замерла is the past feminine of замереть 'to freeze/become motionless'. в этом объятии is a prepositional phrase ('in that embrace'), with этом as the prepositional demonstrative and объятии the prepositional singular of объятие.
segments.p2-9-g506: Но is the adversative conjunction 'but'. все-таки is a compound adverb meaning 'after all' or 'nevertheless' (here split into two words in the annotation: все + таки). штука here is colloquial: 'thing' meaning 'the point/matter'. была is the past tense copula 'was'. в том is an idiomatic locution ('in that') used with 'штука была в том' to mean 'the point was that...' (в + demonstrative том).
segments.p2-9-g507: что is the subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing the content clause. истерика is the nominative subject 'the hysteria' (a sudden hysterical outbreak). должна is the short-form adjective used modally ('must/ought to') and agrees with the feminine subject. же is an emphatic particle stressing inevitability. была is the past tense auxiliary here used with the infinitive пройти to express that the necessity belonged to a past or immediate context ('had to / was bound to'). пройти is the infinitive (perfective) meaning 'to pass / to subside / finish' — i.e., to stop.
segments.p2-9-g508: И вот is a discourse marker roughly 'and so' / 'now then' that introduces what follows. The parenthesis contains я (subject) + ведь (discourse particle 'after all') + омерзительную правду (accusative object; омерзительную modifies правду) + пишу (present tense, 1st person singular of 'to write'), meaning 'I am writing' — the present tense signals an ongoing action or a general fact the narrator asserts while telling the story.
segments.p2-9-g509: лежа is a gerund (деепричастие) from лежать meaning 'lying' and indicates a concurrent state. ничком is an adverb meaning 'face down / prostrate'. да is an archaic/colloquial connective here (in older colloquial style) replacing or standing near the function of a locative connector ('on') before диване; диване is the prepositional singular of диван 'sofa' — together the phrase means 'lying face down on the sofa.'
segments.p2-9-g510: накрепко is an adverb meaning 'firmly' or 'tight', intensifying how he is lying/pressing his face; it modifies the following gerund clause (уткнув лицо...).
segments.p2-9-g511: и is the conjunction 'and'. уткнув is a perfective gerund (уткнув from уткнуть) meaning 'having buried/pressed (one's face)' and indicates an action simultaneous with or immediately preceding the main verb. лицо is the direct object 'face'. в introduces the target of the burying action; дрянную кожаную подушку is the accusative noun phrase 'a lousy leather cushion' (adjectives preceding the noun), and мою is the possessive 'my' placed after the noun for emphatic or literary effect ('my cushion').
segments.p2-9-g512: я is the subject pronoun 'I'. начал is the past masculine singular of начать 'began' (the narrator is male), introducing a new dynamic: 'I began to ...'. помаленьку is an adverb 'little by little' indicating gradualness and modifies the verb phrase that follows.
segments.p2-9-g513: издалека is an adverb meaning 'from afar' or 'at a distance'; here it qualifies the manner in which the feeling arose — indirectly or from a distance (not immediately).
segments.p2-9-g514: невольно is an adverb meaning 'involuntarily' or 'unintentionally' and further qualifies how the sensation came about.
segments.p2-9-g515: но is the adversative conjunction 'but'. неудержимо is an adverb 'irresistibly / uncontrollably' modifying the infinitive ощущать. ощущать is the imperfective infinitive 'to feel / to perceive' and here is the complement of начал (I began to feel); the construction 'начал ... ощущать' = 'began to feel'.
segments.p2-9-g516: что introduces the subordinate clause 'that ...'. ведь is a discourse particle 'after all' stressing the thought. мне is the dative experiencer (impersonal construction) used with the predicative неловко: 'it is awkward for me.' теперь is 'now/from now on'. неловко is a predicative adverb/adjective meaning 'awkward/embarrassed' (socially here). будет is the future tense of 'to be' used impersonally with неловко to mean 'it will be awkward'. поднять голову is an infinitive phrase 'to raise (lit.: lift) my head' with голову in accusative. и coordinates the next action, посмотреть Лизе is 'to look (at) Liza' — Russian uses dative with смотреть/посмотреть to indicate the person looked at when followed by в глаза; прямо 'directly/straight' modifies the phrase в глаза 'in the eyes' (в + accusative plural), giving the collocation 'to look someone straight in the eyes.'
segments.p2-9-g517: Чего is the genitive interrogative form of что, used idiomatically here ('of what' / 'what...of') with the impersonal predicate стыдно. мне is the dative experiencer ('to me'). было is the past tense impersonal copula 'was'. стыдно is the predicative adverb/short adjective meaning 'ashamed' or 'embarrassed' used impersonally with a dative experiencer: literally 'of what was it shameful to me?'.
